title: poa nemoralis var. linnaei
intro: Poa nemoralis var. linnaei, also known as meadow grass or red fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The health benefits of Poa nemoralis var. linnaei include its ability to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome and inflammatory bowel disease, as well as its potential to reduce fever and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used internally to support digestive health and externally to treat skin conditions and wounds.
